title: RPYR-0018 — `_imp.find_frozen(name, <any 2nd arg>)` hits `unimplemented!()` because the guard tests `.is_some()` instead of the value, aborting the VM (stdlib _imp.rs:329)
prior_art: Appears new / unfiled — no fuzzer RUSTPY-* match and no hit on the RustPython tracker for `find_frozen`. A `todo!`/`unimplemented!`-body reached from a Python API (the toolkit treats a reachable `unimplemented!` as CONSIDER, unlike a dead `unreachable!` stub).
found_by: panic-site-auditor (informed explore — NEW CONSIDER, HIGH confidence: guard checks presence not value)
toolkit_class: python-reachable-panic (unimplemented!() reached via a live Python API path)
cpython_behavior: CPython's `_imp.find_frozen` takes exactly one positional arg; a second arg raises TypeError. RustPython accepts a second arg, then aborts on it via unimplemented!().
guarded_twin: The correct behaviour is CPython's own: reject the extra arg (or actually implement the withdata path). The bug is a guard that inspects presence (`.into_option().is_some()`) rather than validating/rejecting the value.
notes: `_imp.find_frozen`'s optional second parameter is guarded by `.into_option().is_some()` — the guard fires whenever a 2nd arg is passed AT ALL (the value is never inspected), reaching an `unimplemented!()` for the withdata code path -> release-build panic-abort (exit 101). Verified live: `import _imp; _imp.find_frozen('_frozen_importlib', True)` -> exit 101; CPython raises `TypeError: find_frozen() takes exactly 1 positional argument`. Trivially reachable (any 2nd arg, even a valid frozen module name). FIX: implement the withdata path, or reject extra args to match CPython's arity. Low-severity (obscure API) but a clean pure-Python interpreter abort.
